creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still a hard crash.

Creating a circular reference (e.g. a map containing itself) and then printing or comparing it causes a Go runtime stack overflow - a hard crash, not a recoverable error.

**Repro:**
m = {}
m["self"] = m
print(m)   // fatal error: stack overflow

a = [1]
a[0] = a
print(a)   // fatal error: stack overflow

No cycle detection exists in print or equality (the value visitor in core/type_visitor.go / type_rad_value.go recurses without a visited-set). Unlikely in normal usage, but a hard crash is always bad.

**Fix:** add a depth guard or cycle detection in print + equality. Pairs with the infinite-recursion crash (both are unbounded recursion crashing Go).
title: Circular data structures cause stack overflow crash
